title: Mulapari Festival at Kariya Kalli Amman Temple – A Celebration of Faith & Nature
excerpt: A heartfelt celebration of faith and nature — the traditional Mulapari festival at Kariya Kalli Amman Temple, where women of the village offered beautifully grown grain pots to the goddess in a vibrant procession of devotion and joy.
author: Siva Shanmuga Vadivel
location: Kariya Kattu Valasu Village, Tamil Nadu

sections.alt: Mulapari Festival at Kariya Kalli Amman Temple

sections.body: Yesterday, our village celebrated the traditional **Mulapari festival** at Kariya Kalli Amman Temple. This festival is a symbol of **fertility, prosperity, and devotion** to our goddess.

sections.alt: Maha Kumbhabhishekam at Kariyakali Amman Temple

sections.body: Mulapari is a traditional Hindu festival where women sow grains in small mud pots, nurture them over several days, and offer the sprouted pots to the goddess as a symbol of abundance and gratitude.
Women of the village sow grains in small mud pots, nurture them, and bring them to the temple as an offering to the goddess.

The festival was filled with **devotion, music, and joy**. Villagers gathered together, dressed in traditional attire, and carried the beautifully grown Mulapari pots in a procession. The temple was decorated with flowers, lights, and the sound of drums filled the air.

Here are some memorable moments captured from the celebration.

*— Siva Shanmuga Vadivel*
